cases.description: Single filer with W-2 wages and early IRA distribution (code 1) — 10% penalty applies, no exception
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Early Distributions lesson (ch. 11, p. 11-11); 10% penalty under IRC §72(t); distribution code 1 per IRS Pub 575; Form 5329 Part I
cases.notes: Early distribution penalty = 10% × $8,000 = $800 (Schedule 2 line 8, Form 5329). Income tax on taxable income $27,250 = 10% × $11,925 + 12% × $15,325 = $3,031.50. Total tax = $3,031.50 + $800 = $3,831.50. Total payments = $2,500 W-2 withholding + $800 1099-R withholding = $3,300.

cases.description: Single retiree, age 75, with RMD (1099-R code 7) and Social Security — no early distribution penalty
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Retirement Income lesson (ch. 11, p. 11-11); Required Minimum Distributions age 73+ per SECURE 2.0; IRC §401(a)(9); Social Security taxable portion per IRC §86; distribution code 7
cases.notes: Provisional income = $22,000 pension + $9,000 (50% of SSA) = $31,000. Between $25,000–$34,000 thresholds: taxable SSA = 50% × ($31,000 − $25,000) = $3,000. AGI = $22,000 + $3,000 = $25,000. Age 65+ single standard deduction = $15,750 + $2,000 = $17,750. Taxable income = $7,250. Tax = 10% × $7,250 = $725. No 10% penalty (code 7).

cases.description: Single filer with W-2 wages and Roth IRA conversion (1099-R code 2) — taxable but no 10% early distribution penalty
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Retirement Income lesson (ch. 11, p. 11-8 to 11-11); Roth conversion taxable per IRC §408A(d)(3); distribution code 2 exempts §72(t) penalty; IRS Pub 590-B
cases.notes: Roth conversion: $15,000 fully taxable (no basis), no 10% penalty because distribution code 2 (early distribution exception applies). AGI = $45,000 + $15,000 = $60,000. Standard deduction = $15,750. Taxable income = $44,250. Tax = 10% × $11,925 + 12% × $32,325 = $1,192.50 + $3,879 = $5,071.50. Total payments = $4,000 + $1,500 = $5,500.

cases.description: Single filer with W-2 wages and Schedule E rental income (net profit, activity type A active participation)
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Schedule K-1 and Rental Income lesson (ch. 12, p. 12-1 to 12-9); rental income on Schedule E per IRC §61, §212, §469; passive activity — active participation allows up to $25,000 loss allowance (income positive here so no limitation applies)

cases.description: Single filer with disability pension income (1099-R code 3) — treated as wages because taxpayer is below minimum retirement age
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Retirement Income lesson (ch. 11, p. 11-9 to 11-10); disability pension below minimum retirement age treated as wages per IRC §105(d) transition; distribution code 3 = disability; reported on Form 1040 line 1a as wages
cases.notes: Disability pension code 3: taxpayer age 42, below employer minimum retirement age — disability income treated as wages on Form 1040 line 1a (not pension line). AGI = $28,000. Standard deduction = $15,750. Taxable income = $12,250. Tax = 10% × $11,925 + 12% × $325 = $1,192.50 + $39 = $1,231.50.

cases.description: Single filer with W-2 wages and 1099-MISC box 3 prizes and awards (game show winnings)
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Other Income lesson; prizes and awards taxable under IRC §74; Form 1099-MISC box 3 other income reported on Schedule 1 line 8z; IRS Pub 525
cases.notes: 1099-MISC box 3 prizes/awards $5,000 added to income via Schedule 1 line 8z. No federal withholding on prizes. AGI = $48,000 + $5,000 = $53,000. Standard deduction = $15,750. Taxable income = $37,250. Tax = 10% × $11,925 + 12% × $25,325 = $1,192.50 + $3,039 = $4,231.50.

cases.description: Single filer with W-2 wages and Schedule H household employment taxes — nanny wages of $12,000
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025; Schedule H household employment taxes per IRC §3101–§3111; FUTA §3301; household employee threshold $2,700 for TY2025; employer and employee FICA each 7.65% × $12,000 = $918; FUTA net 0.6% × $7,000 = $42
cases.notes: Schedule H: employer SS = 6.2% × $12,000 = $744; employer Medicare = 1.45% × $12,000 = $174; employee SS = $744; employee Medicare = $174; FUTA = 0.6% × $7,000 = $42. Schedule H total = $1,878. Income tax on $44,250 taxable income = $1,192.50 + 12% × $32,325 = $5,071.50. Total tax = $5,071.50 + $1,878 = $6,949.50.

cases.description: Single filer with W-2 wages and post-2017 NOL carryforward reducing taxable income (80% limit applies)
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025; NOL carryforward per IRC §172(a)(2); post-2017 NOLs limited to 80% of taxable income (before NOL deduction); carried forward indefinitely with no expiration; Schedule 1 line 8a
cases.notes: AGI = $40,000. Standard deduction = $15,750. Taxable income before NOL = $24,250. Post-2017 NOL 80% limit = 0.80 × $24,250 = $19,400. NOL deduction = min($20,000, $19,400) = $19,400. Taxable income after NOL = $24,250 − $19,400 = $4,850. Tax = 10% × $4,850 = $485. Remaining NOL carryforward = $600.

cases.description: Single filer with W-2 wages and alimony received as income (pre-2019 divorce decree — taxable to recipient)
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Adjustments to Income lesson (ch. 17, p. 17-1); alimony received under pre-2019 decrees taxable to recipient per IRC §71 (as in effect before TCJA); reported on Schedule 1 line 2a; divorce instrument executed before Jan. 1, 2019
cases.notes: Alimony received $12,000 from pre-2019 divorce instrument — fully taxable to recipient (Schedule 1 line 2a). AGI = $32,000 + $12,000 = $44,000. Standard deduction = $15,750. Taxable income = $28,250. Tax = 10% × $11,925 + 12% × $16,325 = $1,192.50 + $1,959 = $3,151.50.

cases.description: Married filing jointly with early IRA distribution (code 1) from non-IRA qualified plan — lower income spouse, exception does NOT apply, penalty assessed
cases.source: IRS VITA Pub 4491 TY2025, Retirement Income lesson (ch. 11, p. 11-11); Form 5329 Part I; IRC §72(t); distribution code 1 from 401(k) employer plan; no qualified exception; Pub 575
cases.notes: AGI = $65,000 + $28,000 + $12,000 = $105,000. MFJ standard deduction = $31,500. Taxable income = $73,500. MFJ income tax: 10% × $23,850 = $2,385 + 12% × $49,650 = $5,958; total income tax = $8,343. Early distribution penalty (code 1, 401k non-IRA, no exception) = 10% × $12,000 = $1,200. Total tax = $8,343 + $1,200 = $9,543. Total payments = $6,000 + $1,800 + $1,200 = $9,000. Amount owed = $543.
